chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-2149 of 2024 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1.For order on office objection. 2.For hearing of CMA No.27690/2024 3.For order on CMA No.18893/2024 4.For hearing of CMA No.22124/2024 5.For hearing of CMA No.20784/2024 6.For hearing of CMA No.9816/2024 7.For hearing of main case. 03.03.2025 Mr.Altaf Hussain, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr.Sandeep Malani, A.A.G. Mr.M.Asif Malik, Advocate for Respondent No.2. SIP Shakeel Ahmed, P.S. Surjani Town is present. Present petition assails the order dated 26.04.2024, whereby the Human Settlement Authority, Government of Sindh had issued de-notification of certain villages. Learned counsel for the petitioners asserts that the de-notification order is mala fide. Learned counsel for the concerned respondent states that the NOC issued with respect to the concerned villages stood withdrawn by the Board of Revenue, hence, no reliance could be placed thereupon because the original notification has also ceased to exist. Learned counsel also refers to the notification issued by Government of Sindh, Land Utilization Department, in respect of the aforesaid issue. Prima facie it is apparent that this petition pertains to assertion of propriety rights in immoveable property and the same cannot be done in writ jurisdiction. Furthermore, the subject matter is fraught with factual controversy, which requires inquiry, investigation and evidence etc., therefore, the same is not amenable for adjudication in writ jurisdiction.

Prima facie it is apparent that this petition pertains to assertion of propriety rights in immoveable property and the same cannot be done in writ jurisdiction. Furthermore, the subject matter is fraught with factual controversy, which requires inquiry, investigation and evidence etc., therefore, the same is not amenable for adjudication in writ jurisdiction. Petition is dismissed. Judge Judge nasir
